changes.check: Authority path — thyroid clinical signoff (mandatory-fresh read)
changes.evidence: docs/Medical Research Documents/thyroid_blood_marker_interpretation_clinical_signoff.md:1-31 — read in full. Signoff covers FT3 high (approved with caveats; TSH-suppressed gate required), FT3 low (blocked — TSH + FT4 + illness context required), FT4 high (approved with caveats; TSH mandatory), FT4 low (approved with caveats; TSH mandatory). CRITICAL: signoff positions TSH as a mandatory GATE marker for the four FT3/FT4 patterns. It does NOT explicitly cover TSH HIGH (primary hypothyroid signal) or TSH LOW (thyrotoxic signal) as standalone primary clinical patterns requiring Medical Review. This creates genuine gate ambiguity at Phase 1A. The formal SOP prompt correctly handles this with a mandatory 1A assessment gate (latest_cursor_prompt.md:295-321): Cursor must determine whether existing signoff is sufficient and STOP with BLOCKED_MEDICAL_REVIEW_REQUIRED if not satisfied.

hardening_findings.classification: ADVISORY — gate correctly placed in prompt
hardening_findings.finding: The thyroid clinical signoff document (thyroid_blood_marker_interpretation_clinical_signoff.md:1-31) covers FT3/FT4 patterns with TSH as a mandatory gate marker. It does NOT explicitly address TSH HIGH (primary hypothyroid pattern) or TSH LOW (thyrotoxic pattern) as standalone primary clinical signal patterns. Whether the existing signoff is sufficient for TSH directional signal activation is a runtime Medical Review judgment call that Cursor must make at Phase 1A. The formal SOP prompt correctly handles this with a hard STOP gate at latest_cursor_prompt.md:295-321. No prompt amendment is required — the gate is present and appropriately framed.
hardening_findings.evidence: docs/Medical Research Documents/thyroid_blood_marker_interpretation_clinical_signoff.md:24 (overall verdict APPROVE_SUBSET — covers FT3/FT4 only); latest_cursor_prompt.md:295-321 (Phase 1A Medical Review gate with BLOCKED_MEDICAL_REVIEW_REQUIRED STOP class)

hardening_findings.finding: crp in Batch_4_Pass_3.json inv_tsh_low spec is canonical. backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml:619 confirms crp is the canonical key. No correction required.

overall_verdict: HARDENED. No blockers. All five front-matter fields confirmed. All authority paths read from source. All eight Stage B GPT amendments confirmed incorporated with no material departure from advisory. Two mandatory Cursor actions flagged (HF-001: ldl → ldl_cholesterol at Phase 1C; HF-002: Medical Review gate judgment at Phase 1A) — both are already gated correctly in the formal SOP prompt and are not hardening blockers. Medical Review gate ambiguity (HF-002) is the highest-risk gate in this sprint; if Cursor cannot confirm existing signoff is sufficient for TSH directional patterns, Phase 1A must STOP with BLOCKED_MEDICAL_REVIEW_REQUIRED before any kb52c opt-in or allowlist change proceeds. Phase 2 must not begin unless all Phase 1 gates pass. Sprint is cleared for kernel start and Cursor implementation.
